seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Coromandel Valley and Corunna Station. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Coromandel Valley vs Corunna Station: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Coromandel Valley and Corunna Station.
heading: Coromandel Valley vs Corunna Station — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Coromandel Valley, South Australia and Corunna Station, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Coromandel Valley has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Corunna Station (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Coromandel Valley or Corunna Station safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Coromandel Valley has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Corunna Station scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Coromandel Valley scores 44/100 for bushfire risk. Corunna Station scores 17/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Coromandel Valley or Corunna Station?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Coromandel Valley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
